title: Critical/High PSIRT Alerting

compliance.assurance_rationale: Tracks Cisco PSIRT security advisories affecting managed devices, providing evidence of vulnerability awareness and remediation tracking as required by NIST 800-53 Flaw Remediation.
compliance.controlObjective: Demonstrates continuous monitoring of vendor-published security advisories for all managed network infrastructure.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search over index=catalyst sourcetype=cisco:dnac:securityadvisory scheduled daily; advisory counts and affected device lists exported to compliance dashboard.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Contributes to vulnerability scanning by tracking known vendor-published vulnerabilities, though it does not replace active scanning tools.
compliance.controlObjective: Provides a vendor-authoritative vulnerability feed for network infrastructure to complement active vulnerability scanning.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Dashboard panel showing advisory severity distribution and affected device counts, refreshed with each TA poll cycle.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Alerts on critical and high severity vendor security advisories, supporting PCI DSS requirements to identify and rank vulnerabilities.
compliance.controlObjective: Ensures critical vendor-published vulnerabilities are flagged within hours of detection by Catalyst Center.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Alert action on UC-5.13.35 firing for severity CRITICAL/HIGH advisories; alert history in triggered alerts log.

dataSources: `index=catalyst sourcetype=cisco:dnac:securityadvisory` from the `securityadvisory` modular input in `TA_cisco_catalyst` (Splunkbase 7538). Same data feed as UC-5.13.34. Filters to `severity IN ("CRITICAL","HIGH")` and `deviceCount > 0` for actionable alerts only.
spl:
index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:securityadvisory" (severity="CRITICAL" OR severity="HIGH")
| stats latest(advisoryTitle) as title latest(cveId) as cve latest(deviceCount) as affected_devices latest(fixedVersions) as fixes by advisoryId, severity
| where affected_devices > 0
| sort case(severity="CRITICAL",1,1==1,2) -affected_devices
description: Fires an alert when a new CRITICAL or HIGH severity Cisco security advisory (PSIRT) is detected that affects devices in your managed infrastructure, providing the advisory title, CVE, affected device count, and available fixes for immediate security response.
value: A new CRITICAL PSIRT affecting your infrastructure is a security emergency. The 2023 IOS-XE Web UI vulnerability (CVE-2023-20198) went from zero-day to mass exploitation in 48 hours. This alert ensures your security team knows about critical advisories within the hourly poll cycle — not days later when the trade press picks it up. The alert payload includes the affected device count and available fixed versions, so the team can immediately assess blast radius and begin emergency patching. For PCI DSS 6.3.1, this demonstrates automated vulnerability identification.
implementation: Same `securityadvisory` input as UC-5.13.34. Schedule as alert: cron `0 * * * *` (hourly), trigger when results > 0. Throttle by `advisoryId` for 7 days. Route CRITICAL to high-urgency page, HIGH to email/Slack.
visualization: (1) Alert payload: advisoryId, advisoryTitle, severity, cveId, deviceCount, fixedVersions. (2) Single value: CRITICAL advisory count (red ≥ 1). (3) Drilldown to UC-5.13.37 (affected devices) for remediation targeting.
knownFalsePositives:
**Critical/High advisory affecting only lab or end-of-life devices.** A CRITICAL or HIGH PSIRT advisory may affect devices that are in a lab environment or have already been scheduled for decommissioning. Distinguish by checking whether the affected `deviceId` values correspond to devices in non-production `siteId` values or devices flagged for EOL. Suppress by maintaining a `catalyst_excluded_devices` lookup and filtering those devices from the critical alerting, while tracking them in a separate risk dashboard.

**Advisory with compensating controls already in place.** The vulnerability may require specific network conditions (e.g., exposed management interface) that are already mitigated by ACLs, segmentation, or other compensating controls. Distinguish by reviewing the advisory's exploitation conditions and verifying whether the compensating controls exist in the network. Suppress by maintaining a `catalyst_advisory_exceptions` lookup with advisoryId, deviceName, compensating control, and review date.

**Catalyst Center severity differs from published PSIRT base score.** Catalyst Center may assess a different severity than the published PSIRT based on local context (device exposure, software configuration). Distinguish by comparing the `severity` field in Splunk with the authoritative PSIRT listing at `https://sec.cloudapps.cisco.com/security/center/publicationListing.x`. No suppression needed — use the authoritative source for final risk assessment.

**Advisory re-detected after Catalyst Center PSIRT database update.** A database update may re-surface previously assessed advisories. Distinguish by checking the advisory's `first_seen` timestamp in Splunk against the database update date. Suppress by tracking `| stats earliest(_time) as first_seen by advisoryId` and alerting only on advisories with `first_seen` within the current reporting window.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• UC-5.13.34 (PSIRT Overview) must be operational — same `securityadvisory` data feed.
• Decide on alert routing: CRITICAL PSIRTs should page the network security lead immediately. HIGH PSIRTs should notify via Slack/email during business hours. MEDIUM and below are informational (covered by UC-5.13.34's weekly report).
• For PCI environments, this alert is part of the vulnerability identification requirement (6.3.1). Document the alert in your PCI controls matrix.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Same `securityadvisory` input as UC-5.13.34. No additional configuration. For fastest detection, consider reducing the poll interval to 1800s (30 minutes) for the advisory input specifically.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
```spl
index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:securityadvisory" (severity="CRITICAL" OR severity="HIGH")
| stats latest(advisoryTitle) as title latest(cveId) as cve latest(deviceCount) as affected_devices latest(fixedVersions) as fixes by advisoryId, severity
| where affected_devices > 0
| sort case(severity="CRITICAL",1,1==1,2) -affected_devices
```

Why `severity IN ("CRITICAL","HIGH")` only: MEDIUM and LOW advisories rarely require emergency response. They're covered in UC-5.13.34's weekly review. Alerting on all severities would cause alert fatigue.

Why `where affected_devices > 0`: advisories that don't affect any device in your inventory are informational but not actionable. Filtering to `> 0` ensures every alert is relevant to YOUR network.

Why `latest()` per advisoryId: deduplicates across poll cycles.

Schedule as Alert:
• Cron: `0 * * * *` (hourly)
• Time range: `-2h to now`
• Trigger: "Number of results > 0"
• Throttle: by `advisoryId` for `7d` (suppress for 7 days — once you know about an advisory, you don't need re-notification)

Step 3 — Validate
(a) Check the Cisco PSIRT portal for recently published advisories. Cross-reference with the Splunk data.
(b) Verify `deviceCount` matches **Catalyst Center > Security Advisories > [advisory detail]**.
(c) Run over 30 days to see historical CRITICAL/HIGH advisory activity.
(d) Throttle test: trigger the alert, then verify it doesn't re-fire for the same advisory within 7 days.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Alerting:
• CRITICAL: high-urgency PagerDuty page to network security lead + `#incident-security` Slack channel.
• HIGH: low-urgency PagerDuty + `#security-ops` Slack channel (business hours only).
• Include: advisoryId, title, CVE, affected_devices, fixes, and link to Cisco PSIRT publication.

Runbook (owner: Network Security):
1. Receive alert. Note severity and affected_devices count.
2. Open the Cisco PSIRT publication URL to read the full advisory detail, including exploitation vectors and workarounds.
3. If fixed firmware is available (`fixes` field): escalate to firmware upgrade team (UC-5.13.56). Create an emergency change request if CRITICAL.
4. If no fix available: apply workarounds from the advisory (typically ACL changes, feature disablement). Track via UC-5.13.38.
5. For internet-facing devices: apply workarounds within 24 hours for CRITICAL, 72 hours for HIGH.
6. Document the response timeline for compliance evidence (NIST SI-2, PCI 6.3.1).

Step 5 — Troubleshooting

• **Alert never fires** — either no CRITICAL/HIGH advisories affect your fleet (good!) or the `severity` field values don't match. Check `| stats values(severity)`.

• **Alert fires for advisories with `deviceCount=0`** — the `where affected_devices > 0` filter is missing. Re-add it.

• **Same advisory re-alerts after 7 days** — throttle expired. This is intentional — if the advisory hasn't been remediated in 7 days, re-alerting is appropriate.

• **`fixedVersions` is empty** — no patch available yet. Apply workarounds and track.

• **Advisory severity disagrees with NVD/CVSS** — use Cisco's severity for operational response (they know their products best). Reference CVSS for compliance reporting.

• **Many CRITICAL advisories appear after Catalyst Center upgrade** — the upgrade may have expanded advisory detection to cover more CVEs. Verify each is genuinely new.

• **Alert action not triggering** — check `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=AlertManager`.

• **Want to differentiate internet-facing vs internal devices** — add a `device_exposure` lookup (deviceName → exposure_level) and route only internet-facing CRITICAL advisories to the emergency channel.
grandmaExplanation: We set up an urgent alarm for when a serious security weakness is found in your network equipment. If a dangerous vulnerability is discovered that affects your devices, your security team gets notified within the hour so they can patch or protect the equipment before attackers can exploit it.
